title: Apply: matter and energy in food webs
description: Apply your understanding of matter and energy in food webs in this set of free practice questions aligned to NGSS standards.
topic_path: Apply: matter and energy in food webs

assessment_items.question_content:
The following food web is from a forest ecosystem.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of these organisms is a producer in the forest ecosystem?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A food web from a forest ecosystem. Arrows point from the persimmon tree to the swallowtail caterpillar, from the persimmon tree to the black bear, from the swallowtail caterpillar to the gray fox, from the gray fox to the bolete fungus (mushroom), from the black bear to the bolete fungus (mushroom), from the silver maple (tree) to the beaver, and from the beaver to the black bear.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following food web is from a tundra ecosystem.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of these organisms is a producer in the tundra ecosystem?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A food web from a tundra ecosystem. Arrows point from the bear sedge (plant) to the brown lemming, from the bilberry (plant) to the brown lemming, from the bilberry (plant) to the arctic fox, from the brown lemming to the short-tailed weasel, from the brown lemming to the the arctic fox, from the short-tailed weasel to the snowy owl, from the snowy owl to the mushroom, and from the arctic fox to the mushroom.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following food web is from a coral reef ecosystem.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Based on the food web, which of these organisms is a producer in the coral reef ecosystem?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A food web from a coral reef ecosystem. Arrows point from the phytoplankton to the damselfish, from the damselfish to the triggerfish, from the damselfish to the reef shark, from the triggerfish to the reef shark, from the reef shark to the bacteria, from algae to the convict tang (fish), and from the convict tang (fish) to the bacteria.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following food web is from a forest ecosystem.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of these organisms is a decomposer in the forest ecosystem?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A food web from a forest ecosystem. Arrows point from the persimmon tree to the swallowtail caterpillar, from the persimmon tree to the black bear, from the swallowtail caterpillar to the gray fox, from the gray fox to the bolete fungus (mushroom), from the black bear to the bolete fungus (mushroom), from the silver maple (tree) to the beaver, and from the beaver to the black bear.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following food web is from a desert ecosystem.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which arrow shows a primary consumer eating a producer?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: An arrow that shows a primary consumer eating a producer will point *away from* the producer, and *toward* the primary consumer.
So, we can answer the question by figuring out which labeled arrow points away from a producer.
Arrow 1 points away from a producer. Arrows 2 and 3 do not.
So, arrow 1 shows a primary consumer eating a producer in the desert ecosystem.

options.alt: A food web from a desert ecosystem. Arrows point from the cactus to the desert woodrat, from the cactus to the coyote, from the coyote to the fungus, from the desert woodrat to the kit fox, from the kit fox to the coyote, from the kit fox to the the fungus, from the creosote bush (plant) to the desert iguana, and from the desert iguana to the kit fox. The arrow from the cactus to the coyote is labeled 1. The arrow from the kit fox to the fungus is labeled 2. The arrow from the desert iguana to the kit fox is labeled 3.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following food web is from a freshwater lake ecosystem.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which arrow shows a primary consumer eating a producer?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: An arrow that shows a primary consumer eating a producer will point *away from* the producer, and *toward* the primary consumer.
So, we can answer the question by figuring out which labeled arrow points away from a producer.
Arrow 2 points away from a producer. Arrows 1 and 3 do not.
So, arrow 2 shows a primary consumer eating a producer in the freshwater lake ecosystem.

options.alt: A food web from a freshwater lake ecosystem. Arrows point from the green algae to the rotifer, from the green algae to the the water flea, from the water flea to the shiner (fish), from the water flea to the bacteria, from the shiner (fish) to the bacteria, from the golden algae to the copepod, from the rotifer to the copepod, and from the copepod to the bacteria. The arrow from the water flea to the shiner (fish) is labeled 1. The arrow from the green algae to the rotifer is labeled 2. The arrow from the shiner (fish) to the bacteria is labeled 3.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following food web is from an ocean ecosystem.

So, we can answer the question by figuring out which labeled arrow points away from a producer.
Arrow 1 points away from a producer. Arrows 2 and 3 do not.
So, arrow 1 shows a primary consumer eating a producer in the ocean ecosystem.

options.alt: A food web from an ocean ecosystem. Arrows point from the kelp (algae) to the kelp bass (fish), from the kelp (algae) the sea urchin, from the sea urchin to the otter, from the kelp bass (fish) to the bacteria, from the otter to the bacteria, from the phytoplankton to the zooplankton, from the zooplankton to the kelp bass (fish), and from the zooplankton to the bacteria. The arrow from the kelp (algae) to the kelp bass (fish) is labeled 1. The arrow from the otter to the bacteria is labeled 2. The arrow from the zooplankton to the kelp bass (fish) is labeled 3.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following food web is from a desert ecosystem.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which arrow shows a secondary consumer eating a primary consumer?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: An arrow that shows a secondary consumer eating a primary consumer will point *away from* the primary consumer, and *toward* the secondary consumer.
So, we can answer the question by figuring out which labeled arrow points away from a primary consumer. Primary consumers eat producers.
Arrow 3 points away from a primary consumer. Arrows 1 and 2 do not.
So, arrow 3 shows a secondary consumer eating a primary consumer in the desert ecosystem.

options.alt: A food web from a desert ecosystem. Arrows point from the cactus to the desert woodrat, from the cactus to the coyote, from the coyote to the fungus, from the desert woodrat to the kit fox, from the kit fox to the coyote, from the kit fox to the the fungus, from the creosote bush (plant) to the desert iguana, and from the desert iguana to the kit fox.
